description_long: This bungalow features two main wooden structures elevated on sturdy stilts, connected by a spacious open deck. The roofs are steeply pitched with ornate gable ends, reflecting traditional Southeast Asian architectural influences. A wide staircase leads up to the deck, providing access to both buildings, each with its own covered area and overhanging eaves. The exterior is uniformly clad in wood, with visible slatted railings around the deck and small, regularly spaced windows along the walls. The overall design emphasizes open-air living, with ample space for movement and gathering on the deck. The structure is raised well above ground level, likely for protection against flooding or to provide ventilation.
description: A traditional wooden bungalow on stilts with two steeply gabled roofs, connected by a large open deck and accessed by a wide staircase.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main staircase, both gabled roofs, and the expansive deck connecting the two structures.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the deck and the alignment of the two buildings, with the gable end of one structure facing forward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the bungalow, the stilts beneath, and the continuous deck connecting both sections.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the secondary staircase and the rear gable, with the deck and railings visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with small, evenly spaced windows
